title: GMAT Data Insights
description: GMAT Focus Data Insights: Data Sufficiency, Two-Part Analysis, Graphics Interpretation, Table Analysis, and Multi-Source Reasoning. Multi-part questions must be fully correct to earn credit.

choices.text: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but (2) alone is not.
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but (1) alone is not.
BOTH together are sufficient, but NEITHER alone is.
Statements (1) and (2) TOGETHER are NOT sufficient.

questions.explanation: (1) $x^2=16 \Rightarrow x=\pm4$ — not sufficient. (2) $x^3=64 \Rightarrow x=4$ only, so $x>0$ — sufficient. Answer: B.

questions.prompt: A group buys exactly 10 tickets for a total of \$74. Adult tickets cost \$9 each and child tickets cost \$5 each. In the table, select the number of adult tickets and the number of child tickets purchased. Make only one selection in each column.
questions.explanation: Let $a$ adult tickets. Then $9a + 5(10-a) = 74 \Rightarrow 4a + 50 = 74 \Rightarrow a = 6$, so children $= 4$. Adult = 6, Child = 4.

questions.prompt: Use the bar chart of quarterly sales to complete each statement.
questions.imageAlt: Bar chart: Q1 20, Q2 35, Q3 30, Q4 45 (thousands of dollars)

parts.prompt: The quarter with the highest sales was
Compared with Q3, sales in Q2 were

questions.explanation: Q4 is the tallest bar at 45. Q2 (35) is greater than Q3 (30).

questions.prompt: Based on the employee data, indicate whether each statement is True or False.

parts.prompt: More than half of the employees work in Engineering.
The employee with the highest salary also has the most years of service.
At least one Sales employee earns more than $80,000.

questions.explanation: Engineering = 2 of 4 (exactly half, not more) → False. Cara has both the highest salary ($92k) and most years (8) → True. Sales salaries are $60k and $55k, neither above $80k → False.

questions.prompt: Using both tabs, indicate whether each statement is True or False.

sources.title: Memo
sources.content: The project has three phases. Phase 2 cannot begin until Phase 1 is complete. The client requires final delivery (end of Phase 3) no later than June 30.

parts.prompt: Phase 2 begins only after Phase 1 is complete.
The project is scheduled to finish on or before June 30.
Phase 3 has the longest duration of the three phases.

questions.explanation: Phase 1 ends ~June 10; Phase 2 starts June 12 → True. Phase 3 ends June 21 + 7 = June 27, before June 30 → True. Durations are 10, 8, 7, so Phase 1 (not Phase 3) is longest → False.

questions.prompt:
What is the value of the integer $n$?

(1) $n$ is a prime number between $20$ and $30$.
(2) $n$ is one less than a multiple of $6$.

choices.text: Statement (1) ALONE is sufficient, but (2) alone is not.
Statement (2) ALONE is sufficient, but (1) alone is not.
BOTH together are sufficient, but NEITHER alone is.
Statements (1) and (2) TOGETHER are NOT sufficient.

questions.explanation: (1) Primes 20–30 are 23 and 29 — not unique. (2) Many values. Together: of {23, 29}, only 23 = 24 − 1 is one less than a multiple of 6 → unique. Answer: C.

questions.prompt: A company's monthly profit is given by $P = 40u - 1200$, where $u$ is the number of units sold. In the table, select the number of units at which the company breaks even (profit = 0), and the number of units that yields a profit of \$800. Make one selection per column.
